summary: Ecclesiastes reflects on the apparent vanity of human striving, wealth, pleasure, toil, and wisdom when considered under the conditions of mortality and time.
historical_context: Traditionally linked with Solomon, Ecclesiastes stands as a philosophical meditation on finitude, frustration, meaning, and reverence for God in a world marked by impermanence.

mirror_water_fire.mirror: Ecclesiastes reflects the emptiness of life pursued as an end in itself.
mirror_water_fire.water: It washes away false confidence in pleasure, labor, wealth, and human control.
mirror_water_fire.fire: The soul is transformed by learning to live humbly before God within the limits of time.
